notes: Requires LLM interaction to test. Prompt-based attack — no static artifact for scanners to analyze.
Requires AI model to be confused into misusing tools. Runtime behavioral test only.
Malformed/inconsistent tool schemas are static artifacts scannable by schema validators.
Command naming conflicts. Can detect via static config analysis but exploitation requires runtime.
CVE-2025-6514 pattern. Client-side vuln may be detectable via dependency scanning.
DNS rebinding attack requires network-level interaction. No static artifact.
Transport-layer attack. Requires intercepting live traffic. No static artifact.
Malicious tool description that shadows legitimate tool. Tool descriptions are scannable static artifacts.
Exfiltration channel may be visible in tool code/config but exploitation requires runtime.
Duplicate/confusing tool names are detectable in static MCP server configs.
Injected instructions in resources. Resource content is scannable but exploitation requires LLM.
Duplicate/confusing server names in MCP configs are statically detectable.
Config changes over time are detectable via static config comparison/analysis.
Escape payloads visible in code but exploitation requires sandbox runtime.
Malicious tool descriptions/params are static artifacts. Core scanning target.
Path traversal and injection vulns in server code are statically scannable.
Server behavior change post-install. Initial config scannable but drift requires monitoring.
